differential_diagnoses: Infective endocarditis (Staphylococcus aureus or viridans streptococci)
Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) with Libman-Sacks endocarditis and neuropsychiatric involvement
Disseminated malignancy with nonbacterial thrombotic endocarditis (NBTE)
Rickettsial infection (e.g., Rocky Mountain spotted fever)
next_steps: Transesophageal echocardiography to evaluate for valvular vegetations
Serologic testing for anti-phospholipid antibodies (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta2-glycoprotein I)
Serologic testing for SLE (ANA, dsDNA, complement levels)
Repeat blood cultures and specialized cultures (fungal, mycobacterial)
Cerebral digital subtraction angiography if vasculitis or mycotic aneurysm is suspected
Serum free light chains and peripheral blood smear review

differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) with Libman-Sacks endocarditis
Primary systemic vasculitis (e.g., Behçet's disease or Polyarteritis nodosa)
next_steps: Transesophageal echocardiography to visualize valvular vegetations
Laboratory evaluation for APL syndrome (lupus anticoagulant, anti-cardiolipin, anti-beta2-GPI)
Laboratory evaluation for SLE (dsDNA, C3, C4, ANA, anti-Smith)
Comprehensive blood and bronchoalveolar lavage fluid cultures for specialized pathogens
Review of peripheral blood smear for schistocytes and platelet morphology
